areas.name: Realisme Data & Domain
evidence: Metric cards show 42 active sessions, 3 attendance review items, and 5 incomplete journals, while the visible table shows only four rows without scope text.
recommended_fix: Add scope copy such as 'Menampilkan 4 dari 42 sesi hari ini' and data freshness text.
acceptance_criteria: Every top metric can be traced to a visible scope, filter, or drill-down.

areas.name: Kejelasan Workflow
evidence: Problem cards communicate issues but do not clearly expose next actions for admin review.
recommended_fix: Add CTAs or clickable drill-downs: Tinjau Absensi, Lihat Jurnal Kosong, Lihat Semua Sesi.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can navigate from each alert to the corresponding filtered worklist.

areas.name: Konsistensi Design System
evidence: Cards, badges, icon style, radius, and spacing are mostly consistent.
recommended_fix: Preserve existing visual system; only tighten any inconsistent chip usage.
acceptance_criteria: No new component variants unless needed for a distinct state.

areas.name: Hirarki Informasi
evidence: Page hierarchy is clear, but Dashboard Overview and Dashboard Admin are mildly repetitive.
recommended_fix: Rename breadcrumb/header label to a more localized and less repetitive pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Primary page title and breadcrumb communicate different levels of context.

areas.name: Copywriting & Lokalisasi
evidence: Mostly Indonesian UI contains English phrase 'Dashboard Overview' and ambiguous '+12 bulan ini'.
recommended_fix: Use consistent Indonesian labels and clarify microcopy such as '+12 pengguna bulan ini'.
acceptance_criteria: All user-facing text follows one language style unless intentionally branded.

areas.name: State & Status Logic
evidence: Status labels 'Hadir', 'Sudah Diisi', 'Perlu Ditinjau', 'Belum Diisi', 'Belum Mengisi', and 'Aktif' overlap. 'Aktif' appears in the time column.
recommended_fix: Separate session status, attendance status, and journal status; standardize label vocabulary.
acceptance_criteria: Each status belongs to a documented category and appears in the correct column or component.

areas.name: Aksesibilitas
evidence: Some pastel badges and small gray text may have weak contrast, and dot color is used as a status cue.
recommended_fix: Improve contrast, add text labels and aria-labels, ensure focus-visible states.
acceptance_criteria: Status remains understandable without color and clickable elements have visible focus states.

areas.name: Layout & Responsiveness
evidence: Desktop layout is good, but right panel and vertical scroll density should be verified at smaller widths.
recommended_fix: Check responsive breakpoints and make right panel stacking intentional.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard remains usable at desktop, tablet, and narrow screen widths.

areas.name: Orisinalitas Visual
evidence: Visual style is polished but still resembles a generic modern admin dashboard.
recommended_fix: Add domain-specific operational details instead of decorative novelty.
acceptance_criteria: Product identity is strengthened through useful academic/admin context.

areas.name: Production Readiness
evidence: No visible filters, loading/empty/error states, or drill-down affordances are shown.
recommended_fix: Add filters, operational links, and states where the component already supports data fetching.
acceptance_criteria: Screen has usable states and controls for real operational use.

summary: The dashboard is visually polished and generally consistent, but shows mild–moderate AI design slop in data scope, workflow actionability, status taxonomy, localization, accessibility, and production readiness.
priority_fixes: Clarify data scope and freshness across metric cards and operational table.
Turn alert cards into actionable review paths.
Add filters, drill-downs, and accessible status/focus states.
